pages.title: Sacramento State College Corps Program | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the College Corps program at Sacramento State.
headings: College Corps Program Division of Student Affairs
About Sacramento State College Corps Program
The Sacramento State College Corps (SSCC) Program
contentBlocks: The Sacramento State College Corps program is where full-time (12 or more units), undergraduate students are connected with local nonprofits and agencies to help community efforts, learn new skills, network, and find out what working in their future career fields might feel like. This program is part of a bigger effort by California Volunteers to encourage students to:
Help with important projects in education, food distribution, health, community and environmental partnerships.
Succeed in school and prepare for their future careers.
Work with local agencies and non-profits to alleviate local issues.
2026-27 Community Host Partners (CHP) Application
Career Center - Sacramento State College Corps (SSCC) Program Lassen Hall, Room 1013 Mon. – Fri. 8 a.m. – 5 p.m.

pages.title: Developing Hispanic–Serving Institutions (DHSI) Career Project (P031S210061) | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the Developing Hispanic-Serving Institution Career Project at Sac State.
contentBlocks: California State University, Sacramento (Sacramento State) has a Title V DHSI Project, “Degree with a Purpose: Integration of Career Development and Financial Wellness into the College Experience.” The DHSI Degree with a Purpose project aims to enhance our existing campus culture of student success by embedding career services and financial wellness into the student experience. Specifically, we develop, implement, and institutionalize three integrated, campus-wide strategies based on research supported by Complete College America’s Purpose First Model and the What Works Clearinghouse evidence-based study (Morisano et al, 2010) on setting, elaborating, and reflecting on personal goals to improve academic performance:

Strategy 1: Early Career Planning and Onboarding Strategy 2: Integration of Career/Financial Wellness Counseling into the College Experience Strategy 3: Integration of Career, Work-Based Learning and Financial Wellness into Courses and Co-Curricular Programs

The proposed project measurable outcomes include: (1) reduce numbers of students who are undeclared; (2) increase retention rates; (3) reduce numbers of students changing majors more than once; (3) increase numbers of students pursuing STEM and in-demand degrees and careers;(4) expand curricular and co-curricular programs with early career development and financial wellness components; (5) increase two- and four-year graduation rates; and (6) earn a Seal of Excelencia , to recognize the institution’s excellence in service to students.
Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment

*Latest Federal Update: The University is currently in year four of a five-year federal DHSI grant. Due to the federal policy shift discontinuing DHSI grants, we did not receive the fifth year of funding. We applied for a no-cost extension, which would allow us to continue using funds from the first four years - without additional funding - through Sept. 30, 2026 or until funding runs out.

links.text: Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment

pages.title: All Featured Resources and Career Development Process | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides Sac State students information about the Career Center's Featured Resources and Career Development Process.
headings: All Featured Resources and Career Development Process
Career Development Process & Featured Tools and Resources
contentBlocks: The career development process is a life-long process of exploring, preparing and engaging with your career stages. The resources below are helpful starting points, although students/alumni are encouraged to make an appointment with staff .
Self-assessment is essential to identifying interests, values, skills and more to achieve a fulfilling career.
Research and gain insights into majors, industries, occupations and roles.
Identify your goals, develop an action plan and start to engage with the industry.
Gain internship, work or volunteer experience related to an industry of interest.
Continue to identify any skills or knowledge gaps for your industry.
Career Development Process & Featured Tools and Resources

pages.title: 15-Minute Drop-In Schedule for Spring 2026 | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information for students on the Career Center's Virtual Drop-In Hours and Services.
contentBlocks: The Career Center is available to assist students and alumni for 15-minute drop-ins to answer quick questions during the hours listed below.
Mondays: 10 - 11 a.m. (Virtual), 11 - 12 p.m. (In-Person) Note: In-person drop-ins will be closed on Monday, 4/13 (virtual drop-ins will be available as normal).
Thursdays: 1 - 2 p.m. (Virtual), 2 - 3 p.m. (In-Person)
Note : Drop-ins will be closed for campus holidays/closures. See list below.

To start your virtual drop-in session, please sign into your EAB account before accessing the above link. Once you add to the queue, please check your Sac State email for a Zoom link. You will be assisted in the order that you added to the queue. Students should join the Zoom link via email immediately to avoid being removed from the queue. To answer more in-depth questions, we encourage you to schedule an appointment. Alumni who are unable to access EAB should contact the Career Center for assistance. Please note: Drop-ins will not take place during holiday campus closures or will be relocated for quick questions at signature events.
January 19, 2026 (Martin Luther King, Jr. Day Holiday, Campus Closed)
March 31, 2026 (Cesar Chavez Birthday Observed Holiday, Campus Closed)
May 25, 2026 (Memorial Day Holiday, Campus Closed)
Please Note: Appointments may be scheduled in person or virtual via EAB Mondays - Fridays during regular business hours.

pages.title: Labor Market Insights | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Labor Market Insights tool assists students with career decision-making.
contentBlocks: The Labor Market Insights tool allows you to look up live labor market trends pulled from real-time data to explore top employment opportunities. Whether you are graduating and searching for a position or just beginning your career exploration journey, this tool has something for everyone!
Desired educational level for each position
List of employers that are currently hiring
Employment trends and predictions over the next ten years
Labor Market Insights Module Tutorial Video

pages.title: Faculty & Staff | Sacramento State
pages.description: Learn more about what services faculty and staff of Sac State's Career Center offer.
headings: Request a Presentation, Speaker or Tabling
Request Staff to Table or Speak at an Event
Connect with the Career Center Team
Best Practices for Collaborating with the Career Center
contentBlocks: Faculty, staff and student club/organization leaders can select from a variety of career-related presentation topics through the “Request a Live Presentation” link below. Live presentations are available during Career Center business hours of 8am – 5pm. Due to the high volume of presentations we receive, please request no more than two presentations for one course.
For presentations outside of business hours of 8am – 5pm, faculty, staff and student club/organization leaders may request a recorded presentation from a variety of topics.
For tabling, panels, and event speaker requests during business hours, please use the form below.
Request Staff to Table or Speak at an Event
Looking for more ways to integrate career development? Check out our best practices for collaboration below! For additional support, please contact careercenter@csus.edu .
Want to partner to embed career development and workforce development components into your curriculum or organization?
The Career Center can also serve as the connection between faculty and employers. Refer community members, and potential employers/internship hosts to our Employer Relations team.
We recommend the following report for information on helping students build momentum toward careers: Purpose First .
Connect with the Career Center Team for Questions

Use career fair and event (Spring Fairs: All Majors Career Fair is Mon., March 2nd and Education, Youth & Family Services Fair is Tues., March 3rd) participation as extra credit or class assignment opportunities (e.g. short reflection)
Add a link to the Career Center website as a student resource in your syllabus or Canvas page.
Assign mock interviews using Big Interview
Encourage students to explore major and career options via FOCUS2 Career and What Can I Do with This Major
Encourage students to research industries that interest them using Labor Market Insights , California Career Zone , O*Net and Library resources
Suggest that students use AI like ChatGPT to check resume and cover letter grammar and spelling
For students in need of extra career support, encourage an appointment for Career Center Counseling.
For other “College to Career Readiness” ideas for a career ready workforce, visit NACE
Join a Professional Learning Community or Teaching Institute in collaboration with CTL on how to help students be Career Ready
Phone: (916) 278-6231 careercenter@csus.edu

pages.title: Hornet Partner Employers | Sacramento State
pages.description: This page provides information about the Hornet Partner Program form employers.
contentBlocks: The following employers are dedicated to recruiting Sac State Hornets for internships and employment opportunities. They offer real-world expertise to our students and alumni, supporting their professional development through mentorship, résumé building, interview preparation, and networking guidance. In addition, these partners share valuable industry-specific insights that help Hornets better understand career pathways and emerging trends in their fields.

pages.title: Career Counseling | Sacramento State
pages.description: Learn about career counseling services for students and alumni.
contentBlocks: The goal of career counseling services at the Career Center is to help students and alumni learn how to make informed decisions in their educational and career choices. Those decisions should reflect students’ personal goals, values, and interests, and should lead toward satisfying academic work in their major and a fulfilling career. Career Counseling Topics
Schedule an Online Appointment - Students/Alumni
Forgot your password? Visit IRT password help.
Instructions to schedule an appointment for students

Sac State Alumni are entitled to 6 career counseling sessions per year, access to events, and career resources. Sac State Alumni who graduated within the last six months, tell us where you are going: First Destination Survey .
Big Interview (Interview & Resume Strategies)

pages.title: Internships | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Career Center is a central hub of information and resources for students seeking internships.
headings: National Association of Colleges and Employers Internship Stats
contentBlocks: Through Internships, students can gain industry experience, develop professional skills, and make valuable connections that could lead to employment in career paths they seek. The Career Center provides services to help students reap the benefits of internships by:
Providing students tools and resources to help them identify and obtain an internship, including those eligible for academic credit
Facilitating employer engagement aimed at increasing internship opportunities
Assisting academic departments and campus programs connect with internship sites
Increase student equity and access to career opportunities by administering the Gaining Access to Internships (GAIN) Fund . The Gain Fund
38.2 days: average time from internship posting to interview
8 months: average time prior to internship start employers begin recruiting
53%: average conversion rate from intern to full-time hire
$19.05: average hourly wage for an intern
Sources: 2024 Internship & Co-op Report and Compensation Guide, Job Offers for Class of 2019 Grads Impacted by Internship Experience, naceweb.org.
15 Best Practices for Internship Programs
Phone: (916) 278-6231 careercenter@csus.edu
Follow us on https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZDFrk6TWpcb-Dm1lfcgoJw?view_as=subscriber

pages.title: Jobs | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Career Center provides resources and support for current students and alumni seeking full or part-time jobs.
contentBlocks: The Career Center assists students with all aspects of conducting your job search, including interviewing techniques, job search strategies, resume and cover letter writing, salary negotiation, professional dress, negotiating offers, and creating a targeted job search plan.
Use your SacLink login and password for access. Alumni requesting access may contact jobs@csus.edu .

pages.title: Employers | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Career Center offers organizations and businesses recruiting services, programs, and events to connect alumni with employment opportunities.
contentBlocks: The Career Center is the central hub for career and employment services at Sacramento State. Working with businesses and organizations of all sizes and from all sectors, we are committed to delivering the very best value for your recruiting efforts.
Finding great Sacramento State students is easy with our free online career services management system, Handshake.
Schedule recruitment events such as career fairs, company presentations (information sessions), on-campus interviews, and table events

Hornet Partners receive exclusive benefits and custom services designed to enhance brand awareness and support hiring goals. The generous support of our Hornet Partners is essential to our ability to provide the robust career services available to all Sac State students and alumni.
Convenience: One payment can cover recruiting expenses for a year
Branding: Benefit from enhanced visibility and targeted marketing
Strategic engagement: Work closely with our team to connect with Sac State talent
Start building your campus network today! Contact
Phone: (916) 278-6231 careercenter@csus.edu
Follow us on https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZDFrk6TWpcb-Dm1lfcgoJw?view_as=subscriber
